failed_change_under_test.summary: Skip _compute_mori_aiter_hot_split_forward on ranks whose local remote-hot row count is zero.
failed_change_under_test.result: Rejected. Hot expert weight broadcast and backward partial dW reduction are all-rank collectives, so a per-rank local empty skip breaks collective order.
failed_change_under_test.code_status: Reverted to all-rank-safe behavior: if any hot experts remain selected, every rank enters _compute_mori_aiter_hot_split_forward even when remote_positions is empty locally.

comparison_reference.read: Helper partial dW reduction back to the hot expert owner already exists and is a moderate communication cost in the working standard-EP branch. It is not the missing multi-second mechanism. The MORI/AITER gap is preserving fused MORI dispatch/AITER expert efficiency while adding helper-side hot-row execution without rank-divergent collectives or tiny route-mask helper calls.
